Class Game

java.lang.Object
scorefour.model.Game
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class Game extends Object implements Runnable
The Game object manages the core logic of the game. This includes game transitions, rendering, and updating game components.

This class initializes various controllers, handles the game loop, and manages the GUI.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    PANEL_HEIGHT
    PANEL_HEIGHT defines the height of the game panel.
    static final int
    PANEL_WIDTH
    PANEL_WIDTH defines the width of the game panel.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    getDebugFPS()
    debugFPS tracks the current frames per second the program is running.
    MenuController
    getMenu()
    The MenuController handles all interactions, visuals, and logic of the games MENU state.
    PlayingController
    getPlaying()
    The PlayingController handles all interactions, visuals, and logic of the games PLAY state.
    void
    render(Graphics g)
    Renders the graphics drawn from the current GameState to a Panel's graphics component.
    void
    run()
    The game loop of the Game object, this method repaints if a panel exists and checks for object updates at a set frame rate.
    void
    startGUI()
    Starts the graphical user interface if a Panel object does not already exist.
    void
    stopGame()
    Stops the game loop from continuing and closes the graphical user interface.
    void
    stopGUI()
    Stops the graphical user interface.

  • Method Details

    • startGUI

      public void startGUI()
      Starts the graphical user interface if a Panel object does not already exist.

    • stopGUI

      public void stopGUI()
      Stops the graphical user interface.

    • stopGame

      public void stopGame()
      Stops the game loop from continuing and closes the graphical user interface.

    • render

      public void render(Graphics g)
      Renders the graphics drawn from the current GameState to a Panel's graphics component.
      Parameters:
      g - the Graphics context used for rendering

    • run

      public void run()
      The game loop of the Game object, this method repaints if a panel exists and checks for object updates at a set frame rate.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • getMenu

      public MenuController getMenu()
      The MenuController handles all interactions, visuals, and logic of the games MENU state.
      Returns:
      MenuController of the Game object

    • getPlaying

      public PlayingController getPlaying()
      The PlayingController handles all interactions, visuals, and logic of the games PLAY state.
      Returns:
      PlayingController of the Game object

    • getDebugFPS

      public int getDebugFPS()
      debugFPS tracks the current frames per second the program is running.
      Returns:
      a integer value of the current frames per second
